evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles validates seedNouns.size() < 2, then delegates to CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles which ALSO validates seedNouns.size() < 2 — duplicate guard on both sides of the facade boundary (SemanticFieldExplorer.java:compareCollocateProfiles and CollocateProfileComparator.java:compareCollocateProfiles).

BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery is a single-line delegation: 'return collocateQueryHelper.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults)' — the interface contract adds no value at this layer, the executor is a pass-through on this method.
BlackLabQueryExecutor.getTotalFrequency is a single-line delegation: 'return collocateQueryHelper.getTotalFrequency(lemma)' — same hollow pass-through pattern as executeBcqlQuery.

FetchExamplesOptions is a single-field options record wrapping int maxExamples. The Javadoc acknowledges this is for binary-compatibility future-proofing, but this is a case where the abstraction cost (extra type, extra indirection) currently exceeds the benefit.

SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed adds only a null-check and delegates entirely to multiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection — thin delegation with minimal policy added. The facade boundary between SemanticFieldExplorer and MultiSeedExplorer is very thin for this path.

QueryExecutor and ExplorationService each have exactly one production implementation; both Javadocs are honest that they exist as testability seams. These are appropriate per the testability-seam skip rule.
ExploreResponseAssembler contains real transformation logic (edge attribution handling for single/multi-seed modes, sharing-category dispatch, rounding) — not a hollow assembler.
RelationPatternBuilder and GrammarConfigSerializer are correctly extracted from their data-carrier records; each has real logic that would otherwise pollute the record types.

SemanticFieldExplorer owns the full single-seed exploration algorithm directly (~150 lines, phases 1–4 with real scoring logic) and only delegates the two orthogonal algorithms to collaborators — a well-calibrated facade that earns its place.
CollocateQueryHelper is a package-private class with substantive two-phase logic (collectHits, scoreHits, buildAndRankCollocates) and frequency caching — not a thin wrapper, a real extraction.
pom.xml declares 5 runtime-scoped dependencies: blacklab-engine, blacklab-query-parser, fastjson2, slf4j-api, logback-classic, and jspecify — all six map to distinct purposes with no overlap.

fastjson2 is used in 6 files (GrammarConfigLoader, GrammarConfigSerializer, HttpApiUtils, CorpusQueryHandlers, VisualizationHandlers, ExplorationHandlers) for both parsing and serialization — not a lodash-for-one-function situation.
slf4j-api is the only logging API imported in source; logback-classic is correctly declared as runtime scope and has zero direct imports in production code — clean facade pattern.
jspecify appears 108 times across @NonNull/@Nullable annotations throughout the API, model, and query layers — usage is broad enough to justify the dependency.

blacklab-query-parser is declared explicitly alongside blacklab-engine; both BlackLabQueryExecutor (ContextualQueryLanguageParser) and CollocateQueryHelper (CorpusQueryLanguageParser) import from nl.inl.blacklab.queryParser.* — the explicit declaration is appropriate for a direct compile-time dependency.
com.sun.net.httpserver (used in 16 locations for the HTTP server) is a JDK built-in, not an external dependency.

Version properties are centralised for blacklab (${blacklab.version}=4.0.0 applied to both BlackLab artifacts) and fastjson (${fastjson.version}=2.0.57), keeping both BlackLab artifacts pinned consistently.
No lock file is present (Maven does not produce one by default), so transitive versions are governed only by BOM-style dependency management of each library.

fastjson2 is an Alibaba-maintained library; while FastJSON2 is a security-improved rewrite, it is less conventional than Jackson (which is the de-facto standard for Java JSON) and may carry more maintenance risk.
webapp/index.html line ~1544 calls `/api/semantic-field?nouns=...&min_logdice=...&max_per_noun=...` — old path with old parameter names, no longer registered on the server
Server (WordSketchApiServer.java) only registers `/api/semantic-field/compare` with `seeds=` parameter; the bare `/api/semantic-field` path has no handler

Webapp checks `data.fully_shared`, `data.partially_shared`, `data.specific` — but API now returns `fully_shared_count`, `partially_shared_count`, `specific_count` (with `_count` suffix), a response-shape mismatch
ExplorationHandlers.java line 131 Javadoc: 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' — stale claim; that route does not exist in the server

RelationType.java: four constants (ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF) are documented as 'legacy labels from an earlier exploration subsystem design'; not set in any grammar config, referenced only in tests
ExplorationResult.java: 5-arg backward-compat constructor delegates to 6-arg; Javadoc on `perSeedCollocates()` says 'Returns an empty map when not available (legacy backward-compat path)'
ExploreResponseAssembler.java lines 46-55: explicit `// Legacy fallback for ExplorationResult instances without per-seed data` branch retained alongside new per-seed path

GrammarConfigLoader.java: `validateNoLegacyKeys()` actively rejects the old `copulas` top-level key with a helpful error; comment at line 159 says 'cql_pattern is no longer supported' and the loader does not accept it — clean migration enforcement

Directory tree has 8 packages: api/ (11 files), config/ (7 files), exploration/ (5 files), model/ (6 files), model/exploration/ (10 files), query/ (6 files), indexer/blacklab/ (3 files), utils/ (4 files) — all below the flat-overload threshold of 10 files except api/ and model/exploration/

api/ contains RadialPlot.java — a public SVG rendering class that is not an HTTP handler; its only production caller is VisualizationHandlers in the same package, suggesting visualization rendering leaked into the HTTP layer rather than being placed in a dedicated viz/ or rendering/ package

GrammarConfigSerializer.java lives in config/ but its test (GrammarConfigSerializerTest) is placed in src/test/.../api/, revealing that contributors already mentally associate serialization with the API layer rather than the config layer; GrammarConfigSerializer depends on fastjson2, an API-tier library, creating a library-level dependency mismatch in config/

RelationEdgeType is placed at model/ root but is exclusively used by model/exploration/Edge.java (as a field type) and api/ExploreResponseAssembler.java; it is exploration-graph-specific and logically belongs in model/exploration/ alongside Edge

api/ package at 11 files mixes four distinct concerns: HTTP routing/infrastructure (WordSketchApiServer, HttpApiUtils, RequestEntityTooLargeException), domain handler groups (SketchHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, VisualizationHandlers), response assembly (ExploreResponseAssembler), and SVG rendering (RadialPlot)
Dependency direction is clean: api → exploration → model; config → model; query → model; no bidirectional or downstream→upstream cycles detected across packages
model/exploration/ sub-package is well-conceived: 10 DTO/value-object types clearly separated from exploration service logic in exploration/; package-info.java documents the design intent thoroughly
RelationUtils lives in config/ and bridges config aliases to grammar-config IDs; it imports model.PosGroup and model.RelationType, which is appropriate fan-out from config → model

BlackLabQueryExecutor declares 'private final String indexPath' and assigns it in the constructor, but no method ever reads this.indexPath — the constructor error message uses the constructor-local variable 'indexPath' directly. The field is a dead assignment.

ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig() checks 'if (relationConfig.isEmpty()) throw ...' then calls 'return relationConfig.orElseThrow()' — the orElseThrow() can never throw because emptiness was already excluded. Calling .get() would express the same intent without the false suggestion of possible absence.

Guard clauses for null/empty inputs are used consistently at method entry across all query executor methods (executeCollocations, executeDependencyPattern, executeSurfacePattern), returning Collections.emptyList() with a debug log rather than silently misbehaving.

The collect→score→rank pipeline in CollocateQueryHelper.executeBcqlQuery is split into clearly named phases with a boolean validCollocate variable that makes the conditional frequency lookup easy to follow.

The multi-seed validation (seeds.size() < 2) appears in three separate layers: HTTP handler, SemanticFieldExplorer facade, and CollocateProfileComparator. Each layer has a slightly different error message. Intentional defense-in-depth but adds noise when reading the call chain.
ternary control flow is used for symmetric cases (headPosConstraint != null ? ... : ...) rather than nested if/else, keeping linear logic paths compact and readable.
The legacy fallback branch in ExploreResponseAssembler.buildExplorationEdges carries an explanatory comment, which the dimension rubric says to skip — intentional design.

RelationType.java: four enum constants (ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF) are explicitly documented as 'legacy labels from an earlier exploration subsystem design... no longer set in any grammar config, but are retained as valid enum constants because some tests and RelationConfig instances reference them' — dead production constants kept alive for test compat

ExplorationResult.java lines 40-44: a public 5-arg constructor (without perSeedCollocates) exists as a compat shim; Javadoc on perSeedCollocates() says 'Returns an empty map when not available (legacy backward-compat path)'; only used in ExploreResponseAssemblerTest.java lines 25 and 34

ExplorationHandlers.java line 131: Javadoc says compare handler is 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' but WordSketchApiServer.java registers only /api/semantic-field/compare — the undocumented legacy path is not registered, making the Javadoc a ghost reference

GrammarConfigLoader.java line 159: comment says 'cql_pattern is no longer supported' but unlike 'copulas', no validation rejects it — a config using cql_pattern silently fails with a misleading 'missing pattern' error rather than a migration hint

RelationUtils.java: RELATION_ALIASES map retains old semantic strings (adj_predicate, modifier, subject_of, object_of) that map to new grammar-config IDs — these serve as live alias resolution for incoming HTTP parameters, not stale shims
QueryExecutor interface Javadoc explicitly distinguishes CQL vs BCQL paths and explains why executeSurfacePattern differs from executeCollocations — honest about what each method does differently
QueryExecutor comment self-declares it is 'primarily a testability seam rather than an abstraction boundary' — interface honesty about its real purpose

ExplorationResult.seed() returns a comma-joined multi-seed string in multi-seed mode despite a singular accessor name; the companion seeds() accessor exists and is documented, but the singular name creates friction for callers reading just the call site

CollocateProfile record names its accessor adjective() and uses field name 'adjective' throughout, but the class is used as a general collocate profile in multi-seed contexts (CollocateProfileComparator is adjective-focused, but the JSON response assembler serializes it as a general 'collocate entry')

SemanticFieldExplorer has two exploreByPattern overloads: the public one takes RelationConfig, the package-private one takes raw pattern strings. The package-private overload is explicitly documented as a 'testing seam' — definition is honest, not hidden

ExplorationResult has a 5-arg constructor that is a backward-compat shim delegating to the 6-arg canonical constructor; the field comment labels it 'backward compat' but the ctor signature itself gives no signal
@NonNull/@Nullable annotations from JSpecify are used consistently on public method boundaries across the api, exploration, and query packages
sealed interface ConcordanceResult permits SnippetResult, CollocateResult provides honest, exhaustive type contract for all concordance result types
RelationType enum and PosGroup enum are used for type-safe dispatch instead of raw strings in config and pattern-building layers

buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution static method is package-private on BlackLabQueryExecutor with a clear contract: only accepts patterns starting with '[', throws IllegalArgumentException otherwise — documented in Javadoc

Class names across all packages precisely predict behaviour: CollocateQueryHelper, BlackLabSnippetParser, ExploreResponseAssembler, CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer all match their implementations exactly.

QueryExecutor interface distinguishes five similar-looking operations by name: executeCollocations, executeSurfacePattern, executeBcqlQuery, executeCqlQuery, executeDependencyPattern — each name accurately differentiates the query strategy used.
Domain vocabulary (logDice, collocate, headword, lemma, deprel, bcqlPattern, seedCollocates, coreCollocates, nounsPerPredicate) is applied uniformly across all layers with no abbreviation drift.

SingleSeedExplorationOptions.collocatesPerSeed() is parsed from the HTTP param 'nouns_per' and assigned in SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern to a local variable named 'nounsPerPredicate'. The API param and the implementation variable both accurately describe the concept (max nouns per collocate in the reverse lookup), but the intermediate Java type field says 'collocatesPerSeed', reversing the relationship — collocates and nouns are swapped.

ConlluConverter.processLines is a private method whose name ('process', generic verb) reveals nothing about what it does: it parses CoNLL-U lines, emits <s>/<\/s> sentence markers, skips comments and multi-word tokens, accumulates token counts, and rotates chunk files.
278 @Test methods across 36+ test files covering API handlers, exploration logic, config loading, query building, response assembly, utils, and viz — no critical in-process path is obviously untested

StubQueryExecutor (src/test/.../query/StubQueryExecutor.java) is a shared, well-structured test double reused by 12+ test classes; all methods return safe empty defaults and are individually overridable via anonymous subclasses

TestExchangeFactory.MockExchange enables testing HTTP handler logic without starting a real server — used consistently across ExplorationHandlersTest, SketchHandlersTest, ConcordanceHandlersTest, CorpusQueryHandlersTest, VisualizationHandlersTest
WordSketchApiServerTest starts a real HTTP server on port 18080, sends real HTTP requests, and verifies routing and lifecycle — the only integration-level HTTP test

BlackLabConllUIndexerTest is @Disabled entirely because it requires an external conllu-sentences.blf.yaml format config not distributed with source — the corpus indexing pipeline, the primary data-ingest path, has zero runnable CI tests

BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest, BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest, BlackLabQueryExecutorTest.LiveIndex, and DependencySketchTest are all @Disabled requiring CONCEPT_SKETCH_TEST_INDEX — the real BlackLab query execution path is never exercised automatically; all query behaviour is validated only through stubs

SemanticFieldExplorerInternalTest accesses the package-private 5-param exploreByPattern overload by living in the same package — this creates test-production coupling: renaming or merging that method signature would break the test even if the public API is unchanged
No snapshot tests found (0%), no timing-dependent tests (no Thread.sleep or ordering dependencies detected), and no reliance on external state in the runnable test suite
Validation/error-path coverage is solid: 400 responses tested for missing seed, one seed, invalid params, unknown relation, missing collocate/noun across all handler classes
model package has zero imports from any other application package — confirmed pure DTO bottom layer; no upward deps from model, query, config, or exploration back to api
query package (CollocateQueryHelper, BlackLabQueryExecutor) imports only from model and external BlackLab library — clean downward dependency
exploration package imports from query, config, model but never from api — correct direction
indexer/blacklab package is entirely self-contained and referenced only from Main.java — no blast radius for server-mode changes
ExplorationService interface in exploration package correctly insulates api from concrete SemanticFieldExplorer class; ExplorationHandlers depends on the interface
QueryExecutor interface in query package correctly insulates api from BlackLabQueryExecutor implementation

VIOLATION: SketchHandlers (api) calls RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern() at lines 206 and 239 — a config-layer pattern-construction call made directly inside HTTP handler logic; pattern building belongs below the API boundary

VIOLATION: ConcordanceHandlers (api) calls RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern() at line 59 — same cross-boundary call as SketchHandlers; two separate api-layer handlers reach into config internals to construct CQL strings

PARTIAL CONCERN: ExplorationHandlers (api) imports GrammarConfig, RelationConfig, RelationUtils from config to perform HTTP-param-to-domain-object resolution (resolveRelationConfig method); this tightly couples the api layer to config record types and alias maps

WordSketchApiServer convenience constructor (line ~57) directly instantiates SemanticFieldExplorer by concrete class name rather than using only the ExplorationService interface — the full constructor correctly accepts ExplorationService, but the production convenience path re-couples the api package to the concrete implementation
No cycles detected: dependency graph is a strict DAG — Main → api/indexer/query/config; api → exploration/query/config/model; exploration → query/config/model; query → model; config → model/utils
Package size distribution is balanced: api 1745 lines, query 1035, exploration 756, config 683, model 627, indexer 271 — no God package

strengths: QueryExecutor interface Javadoc is a model of honest single-implementation documentation: it names the single implementor, explains the testability-seam rationale, and explicitly records that a split is deferred — a developer never wonders why the interface exists
API handler decomposition into five focused package-private classes assigns each a narrow, non-overlapping HTTP concern; no handler leaks responsibility into another
ExploreResponseAssembler is a genuine translation layer — every method performs real domain-to-presentation conversion rather than forwarding fields wholesale
Config concern separation across four distinct classes (Loader / Serializer / Config record / PatternBuilder) is clean: each class has exactly one reason to change
Mechanical scan found zero delegation-heavy classes and zero facade/re-export modules, consistent with what direct code reading confirms
abstraction_fitness.issue_character: Issues are localized to the exploration subsystem: two of four ExplorationService interface methods in SemanticFieldExplorer are pure forwarding delegations that unwrap an options record and pass scalars to inner objects, and ExplorationService's own documentation does not explain the design tradeoff the way QueryExecutor's does — a narrow pattern in one facade class, not a codebase-wide problem.
abstraction_fitness.score_rationale: The codebase sits clearly above the 80 'solid but uneven' anchor: abstractions are mostly purposeful, the API layer decomposition is tight, and the two single-implementation interfaces are explicitly documented as testability seams. The primary drag is SemanticFieldExplorer acting as a thin facade for exploreMultiSeed and compareCollocateProfiles — these two methods forward to inner objects after trivial parameter unpacking, adding a navigation hop without contributing behavior, and ExplorationService's documentation omits the rationale that would make this intentional to a new reader. This is a localized concern in one class, not systemic, which keeps the score well above 80 but below the 90 'strong' range where all indirection layers clearly pay for themselves.

strengths: Lean dependency footprint: only 5 runtime dependencies covering corpus search (BlackLab), JSON (fastjson2), logging (slf4j+logback), and null-safety (jspecify) — nothing redundant.
No duplicate libraries for the same purpose: single JSON library, single logging facade, single logging implementation.
logback-classic correctly declared as runtime scope, preserving the slf4j abstraction with zero direct logback API calls in production code.
Version properties centralised for both BlackLab artifacts (4.0.0) preventing version skew between blacklab-engine and blacklab-query-parser.
jspecify usage is pervasive (108 annotation sites) justifying its inclusion as a purposeful, lightweight annotation library.
dependency_health.issue_character: A single stylistic concern — fastjson2 instead of the more conventional Jackson — is the only real finding; all other dependency hygiene signals are positive.
dependency_health.score_rationale: The dependency graph is clean: every declared runtime dependency has a clear, broad role, versions are consistent, no duplication, and the logging facade is used correctly. The sole meaningful deduction is fastjson2 — an Alibaba-backed library rather than the Java-ecosystem-standard Jackson — which introduces a mild long-term maintainability and security-optics risk without providing a compelling advantage over Jackson at the same API surface (JSON.parseObject / JSON.toJSONString). This places the codebase solidly in the 80-85 range: strong overall dependency hygiene with one conventional-choice gap.

strengths: `GrammarConfigLoader.validateNoLegacyKeys()` actively rejects the deprecated `copulas` config key with a clear error message, preventing silent use of old config shapes
The `cql_pattern` → `pattern` field rename is fully enforced: the old name is rejected, not silently accepted, ensuring no dual-path ambiguity in config loading
Legacy `RelationType` constants (ADJ_PREDICATE etc.) are explicitly documented as retained only for tests, and the production priority-hint logic that used them has been removed
Server route table is clean with no duplicate legacy/new routes registered for the same functionality — migration residue is restricted to documentation and model backward-compat
incomplete_migration.issue_character: A single concrete broken UI feature exposes an incomplete API migration: the webapp's 'Compare noun profiles' section still calls the old endpoint path and parameter names, while the server was migrated to a new path/params without updating the client — resulting in a live 404 and a response-shape mismatch.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The backend code is well-migrated: deprecated config keys are actively rejected, old API field names are cleanly removed, and legacy code paths are clearly labeled. This pulls the score above 60. However, the webapp still calls the pre-migration `/api/semantic-field` endpoint with old parameters (`nouns=`, `max_per_noun=`) and expects old response field names (`fully_shared` rather than `fully_shared_count`), which represents a live broken feature. A stale Javadoc in ExplorationHandlers claiming the old path is 'also reachable' compounds the confusion by implying intentional backward compatibility that does not exist. The ~80 anchor ('old and new patterns coexisting, making it unclear which to follow') fits the doc/client mismatch, but the backend itself is cleaner than that, settling the score at 68.

strengths: Clean layered dependency direction: api → exploration → model, config → model, query → model with no cycles or back-edges observed
model/exploration/ sub-package correctly isolates 10 exploration-specific DTOs from general model types, matching change-boundary intuition
All packages have package-info.java files that accurately describe their responsibilities, aiding navigation
exploration/ExplorationService interface lives in the domain package, not api/, enabling proper inversion between HTTP and domain layers
Directories are generally right-sized: most packages are 3–7 files with clear cohesion; config/ (7 files) covers loading, parsing, serialization, and pattern building as one coherent unit
package_organization.issue_character: The issues are isolated misplacements rather than systemic boundary failures: one public rendering class (RadialPlot) accumulated in the HTTP handler package, one serializer (GrammarConfigSerializer) sitting in its data package despite having HTTP-layer affinity, and one enum (RelationEdgeType) stranded at the model root instead of its exploration subpackage.
package_organization.score_rationale: The overall structure is solid and navigable: layered packages, meaningful names, correct dependency direction, and no coupling-direction failures. What pulls the score below 80 is the api/ package mixing SVG rendering (RadialPlot) with HTTP handlers — a developer looking for visualization logic would not naturally start in the api/ package — and GrammarConfigSerializer's placement in config/ despite its test living in api/ and its dependency on the JSON HTTP library, creating mild cognitive friction for anyone maintaining the serialization path. These are ownership-clarity issues with measurable navigation impact but do not represent architectural dysfunction, putting the score in the 70s rather than the 80s.

strengths: Guard-clause pattern used consistently at entry points of all six query methods — null/empty inputs produce clean early returns rather than propagating into broken state.
Multi-phase algorithms (collect → score → rank) are decomposed into named helpers with clear sequential intent, making the control flow in executeBcqlQuery and SemanticFieldExplorer easy to trace.

Conditional branches are semantically asymmetric throughout: every if/else encodes a genuinely different outcome (fallback vs resolved, present vs absent, valid vs invalid), with no identical-branch duplication found.
Named boolean variables (e.g. 'boolean validCollocate', 'boolean fallback') are extracted from complex conditions before they are used, avoiding nested boolean expressions inline.
logic_clarity.issue_character: Issues are isolated dead-code artefacts and a mild Optional misuse — there are no systemic logic defects, just two small spots where the code says more than it needs to.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: The codebase sits comfortably in the 75–80 range. Control flow is provably correct in every path examined: no dead paths after returns, no always-true/false guards, no duplicate branches. The two concrete findings — a never-read field and a redundant orElseThrow after an explicit guard — are local and non-functional, but they do leave subtle misleading signals for readers. The three-layer duplication of the min-2-seeds validation is not a bug but adds navigational noise. These issues push the score slightly below 80 (solid but uneven) without approaching the 60 range (significant drag).

strengths: GrammarConfigLoader.validateNoLegacyKeys() actively throws when the old 'copulas' top-level key is present, enforcing that the deprecated config format cannot silently persist
All production callers of ExplorationResult use the canonical 6-arg constructor; the 5-arg compat shim is confined to test helpers only
RelationType.java clearly documents the 4 legacy constants as non-production artifacts, making them discoverable rather than silently dead
No @Deprecated-annotated methods exist in production code that are still called by active code — the annotation hygiene is clean
RELATION_ALIASES in RelationUtils is still actively used for legitimate URL parameter resolution, not a dead shim
stale_migration.issue_character: Issues are isolated, well-documented residuals from a completed migration rather than an ongoing or neglected one — a ghost Javadoc path, four dead enum constants, and one compat constructor kept for test convenience.
stale_migration.score_rationale: The codebase has completed its migration from the old semantic-role RelationType design to the grammar-config-driven pattern, and has documented the residuals clearly. No deprecated symbols are silently called by production code, and the loader enforces format hygiene. The score is pulled down by the four dead RelationType enum constants that the grammar config can never set (making findBestCollocatePattern's primaryTypes logic always fall through to the fallback), a Javadoc ghost that references an unregistered legacy API path, and a 5-arg ExplorationResult constructor kept alive only for test convenience. These are manageable isolated artifacts consistent with a score in the high-70s range on the global anchor scale — better than 'significant drag' (60) but short of 'solid but uneven' (80) due to the ghost Javadoc and dead enum constants.

strengths: QueryExecutor interface is unusually self-aware: it explicitly documents why each method exists, how they differ from each other, and that it is a testability seam rather than an architectural abstraction — a developer can trust the interface at face value
JSpecify @NonNull/@Nullable annotations are applied consistently on public API boundaries across all packages, making nullability contracts part of the definition rather than implicit
sealed interface ConcordanceResult with explicit permits creates an honest, compiler-enforced type contract for the two concordance result kinds

ExplorationService interface and its methods honestly represent their contracts — exploreByPattern, exploreMultiSeed, and compareCollocateProfiles each carry docstrings that match their behavior and call out design trade-offs explicitly
Package-info.java files in api, config, exploration, model, and indexer.blacklab establish clear package-level contracts that match what is actually in each package
definition_directness.issue_character: Issues are isolated naming mismatches — a singular accessor masking plural behavior and an adjective-specific vocabulary applied to a general-purpose type — rather than systemic dishonesty. No mismatched return types, hidden side effects, or fabricated contracts.
definition_directness.score_rationale: The codebase lands solidly above the 80-anchor (solid-but-uneven): interfaces are self-documenting, type contracts are explicit and honest, and abstraction layers generally earn their keep. Two isolated frictions keep it below 85: ExplorationResult.seed() implies a single value but returns a comma-joined multi-seed string in multi-seed mode, and CollocateProfile names its accessor adjective() throughout even when used as a general collocate type outside the adjective-comparison context. Both are documented but create call-site confusion. The many strengths — sealed types, nullable annotations, honest single-impl interface documentation — prevent this from dropping below 80.

strengths: Package and class names precisely predict behaviour throughout — CollocateQueryHelper, BlackLabSnippetParser, ExploreResponseAssembler, CollocateProfileComparator are all verifiably accurate.

QueryExecutor's five execute* methods are distinguishable by name alone (executeCollocations vs executeSurfacePattern vs executeBcqlQuery vs executeCqlQuery vs executeDependencyPattern) despite sharing a prefix.
Rich, consistent domain vocabulary (logDice, headword, collocate, lemma, deprel, bcqlPattern) is applied uniformly across all layers — no local dialects or abbreviation drift.
Model records closely describe content: DiscoveredNoun, CoreCollocate, CollocateProfile, WordSketchResult, ExplorationResult all match what they hold.
Internal variable names track semantic roles precisely: headwordFreq, lemmaPosMap, freqMapOut, seedCollocateScores, collocateSharedCount.
naming_quality.issue_character: Issues are isolated rather than systemic: one vocabulary inversion (collocatesPerSeed names the opposite direction from the concept it controls) and one generic-verb private method (processLines); the rest of the codebase demonstrates strong naming discipline.
naming_quality.score_rationale: The codebase sits clearly above the 80-anchor ('regularly encounters names that don't communicate intent'): the vast majority of names are precise and a developer can read class and method names and predict behaviour without opening files. The `collocatesPerSeed` mismatch is the most consequential — it inverts the collocate/noun relationship in the public options type, which would mislead a developer tuning the single-seed exploration algorithm. The `processLines` generic verb is a real but local friction point. Together these two issues create occasional confusion that prevents reaching the 90-anchor ('names are mostly precise; only a few require a second look').

strengths: Shared StubQueryExecutor with safe defaults and per-test anonymous-subclass overrides is consistently used across 12+ test classes, making unit tests easy to write and maintain without implementation coupling
HTTP handler layer is fully unit-testable via MockExchange; every handler class has a corresponding test class with both happy-path and error-path coverage
WordSketchApiServerTest spins up a real HTTP server, covering server lifecycle and route registration end-to-end — genuine integration coverage for the API wiring
Disabled live-index tests are clearly documented with exact instructions to re-enable, so they serve as a runnable specification for when an index is available
No fragility patterns: zero snapshot tests, zero timing dependencies, zero reliance on external state in the runnable test suite
test_strategy.issue_character: The deficiencies are concentrated at the external-dependency boundary: the two critical paths that require a real BlackLab index (corpus indexing via ConllUIndexer; live query execution via BlackLabQueryExecutor) cannot be exercised in CI, so automated test coverage has a structural gap precisely where correctness matters most.
test_strategy.score_rationale: The in-process coverage is genuinely strong — 278 tests, no fragility, good error-path coverage, well-designed stubs — which places the codebase above the 60 'significant drag' anchor. However, both the data-ingest path (indexer) and the live query path (BlackLabQueryExecutor + BlackLab integration) are entirely behind @Disabled gates that cannot be lifted in CI, meaning core business functionality is validated only through stubs. There is also mild test-production coupling in SemanticFieldExplorerInternalTest via package-private access. These gaps prevent reaching the 80 'solid but uneven' tier, landing the codebase at 68: strong unit strategy but a notable structural hole at the integration boundary.

strengths: model package is a clean bottom layer with no outward deps — all data flows down to it, never up from it
QueryExecutor and ExplorationService interfaces establish real isolation seams between the api layer and the concrete implementations in query and exploration
indexer/blacklab is fully isolated from the runtime server path — only Main.java references it, giving it zero blast radius for API changes
No circular dependencies anywhere in the package graph
Package sizes are well-balanced; no single package dominates the codebase
cross_module_architecture.issue_character: The architecture has a sound layered skeleton, but the api layer repeatedly reaches across its boundary into config internals (RelationPatternBuilder) to do work that logically belongs in a service or query layer — a consistent inside-out coupling pattern rather than isolated one-off leaks.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The codebase scores above the midpoint because the core architectural contracts are honoured: no upward dependencies from lower layers, two proper interface seams (QueryExecutor, ExplorationService), a clean model bottom layer, and no cycles. It is docked for the recurring api→config boundary violation (SketchHandlers and ConcordanceHandlers both call RelationPatternBuilder directly) and for the convenience constructor in WordSketchApiServer naming the concrete SemanticFieldExplorer. These are real violations that would force api-layer changes whenever pattern-building logic evolves, but they affect a narrow part of the call graph and are straightforwardly fixable by introducing a query-construction service method or moving pattern-building behind the QueryExecutor or ExplorationService interfaces.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed and compareCollocateProfiles are parameter-unwrapping pass-throughs, and ExplorationService omits the design rationale
issues.suggestion: Add a design-rationale paragraph to ExplorationService (mirroring QueryExecutor's Javadoc) explaining that the interface exists as a testability seam and that SemanticFieldExplorer is the only production implementation. Separately, consider whether exploreMultiSeed and compareCollocateProfiles belong on SemanticFieldExplorer at all: if the facade's only role for those two methods is parameter unpacking, the ExplorationHandlers could hold references to MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator directly, or ExplorationOptions could expose a method to unpack into the inner call so the delegation line is one expression rather than three arguments.
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreMultiSeed (line ~356) is a single-line method: it unwraps opts.minLogDice(), opts.topCollocates(), opts.minShared() and forwards directly to multiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection — no added behavior, guard, or policy

SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles (line ~315) is similarly a direct forward to comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(seedNouns, opts.minLogDice(), opts.topCollocates()) after unwrapping the options record

ExplorationService Javadoc reads only 'Implemented by SemanticFieldExplorer' — unlike QueryExecutor which has a full paragraph explaining the testability-seam rationale and deferral decision, ExplorationService gives a developer no signal that the single-impl is intentional

issues.summary: fastjson2 (Alibaba) used for JSON instead of the ecosystem-standard Jackson
issues.suggestion: Replace fastjson2 with Jackson Databind (com.fasterxml.jackson.core:jackson-databind). The API surface used is minimal: swap JSON.parseObject(str) with objectMapper.readValue(str, Map.class) and JSON.toJSONString(obj) with objectMapper.writeValueAsString(obj). Check whether Jackson is already present as a transitive dependency via BlackLab before adding a new explicit declaration.
evidence: pom.xml: <artifactId>fastjson2</artifactId> <version>2.0.57</version> — Alibaba library chosen over Jackson Databind
Only two API entry points are used across the entire codebase: JSON.parseObject() and JSON.toJSONString() — both have direct Jackson equivalents (ObjectMapper.readValue / writeValueAsString)
FastJSON (predecessor) had multiple critical RCE CVEs; FastJSON2 is a rewrite but the Alibaba JSON lineage is less trusted than Jackson in security-sensitive contexts
Jackson-databind is already a transitive dependency of BlackLab (common in Lucene-ecosystem libraries), meaning fastjson2 may add a second JSON library to the classpath unnecessarily

issues.summary: webapp calls removed `/api/semantic-field` endpoint with old params and expects old response field names
issues.suggestion: Update webapp/index.html 'Compare noun profiles' fetch call to use the canonical path `/api/semantic-field/compare` with the `seeds=` parameter in place of `nouns=`, remove the `max_per_noun=` parameter (now unused), and update the three stat reads from `data.fully_shared` / `data.partially_shared` / `data.specific` to `data.fully_shared_count` / `data.partially_shared_count` / `data.specific_count`. Also remove the stale Javadoc clause 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' from `ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldComparison`.
evidence: webapp/index.html ~line 1544: `fetch(`${apiUrl}/api/semantic-field?nouns=...&min_logdice=...&max_per_noun=...`)` — old endpoint path, old `nouns=` param (now `seeds=`), old `max_per_noun=` param (not accepted)

WordSketchApiServer registers only `/api/semantic-field/compare`, `/api/semantic-field/explore`, `/api/semantic-field/explore-multi`, and `/api/semantic-field/examples`; bare `/api/semantic-field` is absent
Webapp checks `data.fully_shared`, `data.partially_shared`, `data.specific` but ExploreResponseAssembler now puts these under `fully_shared_count`, `partially_shared_count`, `specific_count`
ExplorationHandlers.java line 131 Javadoc says the compare endpoint is 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' — misleading because that route is not registered

issues.summary: RadialPlot (SVG renderer) lives in api/ despite being a pure rendering class with no HTTP concerns
issues.suggestion: Move RadialPlot to a new api/viz/ subpackage (or a standalone viz/ package if it is expected to be reused outside the API layer). Steps: (1) create src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/api/viz/RadialPlot.java with package api.viz; (2) update VisualizationHandlers to import api.viz.RadialPlot; (3) verify with mvn compile; (4) move the corresponding test if one exists. The package-info for api/ should be updated to exclude 'rendering' from its stated scope.
evidence: RadialPlot is declared public with no package-private visibility, suggesting cross-package reuse was anticipated, but it has no importers outside api/
RadialPlot contains only layout math and SVG string building — no HttpExchange, no HTTP headers, no routing — yet it resides alongside ConcordanceHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, etc.

VisualizationHandlers.java calls RadialPlot.renderFromItems() — the HTTP handler is a thin wrapper over the renderer, which is the correct pattern, but the renderer should live in a separate package (e.g. api/viz/ or a top-level viz/)

issues.summary: GrammarConfigSerializer resides in config/ despite having HTTP-layer affinity and its test living in the api test package
issues.suggestion: Move GrammarConfigSerializer to api/ (or api/serialization/ if further separation is desired) since it is an API-layer concern: it converts config value objects into HTTP response payloads. Steps: (1) move file to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/api/GrammarConfigSerializer.java; (2) update its package declaration; (3) update callers (grep for GrammarConfigSerializer imports); (4) move GrammarConfigSerializerTest to the matching api test directory (it may already be there); (5) run mvn test to confirm. Update config/package-info.java to remove 'serialization' from its listed scope.
evidence: GrammarConfigSerializer imports com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSONObject and JSONArray — library dependencies shared with the api/ HTTP layer — while config/ otherwise has no JSON library dependency
The test class GrammarConfigSerializerTest is placed in src/test/java/.../api/, not src/test/java/.../config/, indicating the contributors' mental model aligns it with the API layer
GrammarConfigSerializer's sole purpose is to produce JSONObject representations for API responses and diagnostic output (per its own Javadoc)

issues.summary: RelationEdgeType lives at model/ root but is exclusively used by model/exploration/ and api/ exploration assembly
issues.suggestion: Move RelationEdgeType to model/exploration/: (1) relocate to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/model/exploration/RelationEdgeType.java; (2) update the package declaration; (3) update imports in Edge.java and ExploreResponseAssembler.java (two files); (4) run mvn test. This is a low-risk, single-pass import update.
evidence: RelationEdgeType is a field type on model/exploration/Edge.java — its primary structural home is the exploration graph model
All three usages (Edge.java, ExploreResponseAssembler.java, ExploreResponseAssemblerTest.java) are exploration-graph-specific; no non-exploration callers exist
Placing it at model/ root suggests it has general applicability, but its enum values (SEED_ADJ, DISCOVERED_ADJ, MODIFIER) are exploration-domain vocabulary

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor stores indexPath in a field that is never read after construction
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'private final String indexPath' field declaration and the 'this.indexPath = indexPath' assignment. The constructor parameter is only ever needed inside the constructor (for the BlackLab.open call and the error message), so storing it as a field adds noise without benefit.
evidence: Line 48: 'private final String indexPath;' is declared
Line 61: 'this.indexPath = indexPath;' assigns the constructor argument to the field
The constructor error message at line 68 uses the constructor-local variable 'indexPath' not 'this.indexPath', and no other method in the class references 'this.indexPath'
The field is present but inert — a reader scanning the class will wonder where indexPath is used without finding any answer

issues.summary: resolveRelationConfig returns relationConfig.orElseThrow() after an explicit isEmpty() guard that already excludes the empty case
issues.suggestion: Replace 'return relationConfig.orElseThrow()' with 'return relationConfig.get()' to express directly that the value is known to be present at this point. This removes the false implication of possible absence and makes the control flow self-evident.
evidence: Lines 257-265: the method checks 'if (relationConfig.isEmpty()) throw new IllegalArgumentException(...)' and then returns 'relationConfig.orElseThrow()'
After the isEmpty guard, the Optional is provably non-empty, so orElseThrow() can never throw its NoSuchElementException

A reader encountering orElseThrow() at the return site will wonder what exception it would throw and when — implying the Optional might still be absent even though the guard above makes that impossible

issues.summary: Four legacy semantic-role RelationType constants are never set by any grammar config but remain as live production enum values
issues.suggestion: Remove ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF from RelationType — they add no active dispatch value. Update the three test usages in RelationPatternBuilderTest (lines 120, 128, 136) and RelationUtilsTest (lines 61, 65) to use SURFACE or DEP or remove those test cases if they are testing dead branches. Also remove the primaryTypes vararg from findBestCollocatePattern in RelationUtils if no caller ever passes non-empty primary types.
evidence: RelationType.java lines 23-29: ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF — Javadoc explicitly states 'Not set in grammar config'

RelationUtils.findBestCollocatePattern accepts RelationType... primaryTypes but no production caller passes these constants — SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator call it with no varargs, so the primary-type filter never matches and the fallback always fires

issues.summary: Javadoc documents a legacy API path /api/semantic-field that is not registered in WordSketchApiServer
issues.suggestion: Remove the sentence 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' from ExplorationHandlers.java handleSemanticFieldComparison Javadoc and update the ExploreResponseAssembler.java line 142 comment to reference /api/semantic-field/compare instead.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.java line 131: 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field'
WordSketchApiServer.java registerRoutes(): only /api/semantic-field/compare is registered; no registration for bare /api/semantic-field path
ExploreResponseAssembler.java line 142: comment references '/api/semantic-field endpoint' without the /compare sub-path, suggesting both files document a path that no longer exists

issues.summary: Public 5-arg ExplorationResult constructor is a backward-compat shim used only in tests, not in production code
issues.suggestion: Remove the 5-arg constructor from ExplorationResult and update ExploreResponseAssemblerTest to pass Map.of() as the sixth argument. This eliminates the compat overload and makes the required perSeedCollocates field explicit at all construction sites.
evidence: ExplorationResult.java lines 40-44: 5-arg constructor without perSeedCollocates, delegates to 6-arg with Map.of()
ExplorationResult.java perSeedCollocates() Javadoc: 'Returns an empty map when not available (legacy backward-compat path)'
All production callers (MultiSeedExplorer.java line 77, SemanticFieldExplorer.java line 202) use the 6-arg constructor; only ExploreResponseAssemblerTest.java lines 25 and 34 use the 5-arg form

issues.summary: ExplorationResult.seed() returns a comma-joined string in multi-seed mode despite singular accessor name
issues.suggestion: Rename seed() to seedLabel() or displaySeed() to signal that it is a derived display string, not a raw lemma value. Alternatively, deprecate seed() in favor of seeds() for code paths that need the list and make seed() private or package-private so the mismatch is contained.
evidence: ExplorationResult.seed() is documented as returning a 'combined display string' that is comma-joined in multi-seed mode (e.g. 'theory,model,hypothesis'), but the method is named seed() — singular — suggesting a single lemma to any reader who does not inspect the Javadoc

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldExplore calls result.seed() and puts it in response.put("seed", result.seed()), which works correctly in single-seed mode but would produce a comma string in multi-seed mode if ever reached on that path

issues.summary: CollocateProfile names its accessor adjective() but the type is used as a general collocate profile beyond adjective-only contexts
issues.suggestion: Rename the field and accessor from adjective to collocate (consistent with the class-level comment 'Profile of one collocate') and update all call sites: CollocateProfile.adjective() → CollocateProfile.collocate(). This is a mechanical rename; no behavior changes.
evidence: CollocateProfile record defines String adjective as its primary key field with accessor adjective(), and the class comment says 'Profile of one collocate' — indicating the designer was already aware the type is general
ExploreResponseAssembler.formatCollocateProfile serializes the adjective() accessor as the 'word' key, adding an implicit translation step to paper over the vocabulary mismatch
The type is used inside ComparisonResult.collocates() and the serialized response key is 'word', not 'adjective' — but calling code still reads .adjective() at definition sites

issues.summary: SingleSeedExplorationOptions.collocatesPerSeed names the nouns-per-collocate parameter as if it were collocates-per-seed
issues.suggestion: Rename the field and accessor to nounsPerCollocate (or nounsPerPredicate) in SingleSeedExplorationOptions and the matching field in ExplorationHandlers.ExplorationParams. Update construction sites in ExplorationHandlers.parseExplorationParams and handleSemanticFieldExplore accordingly.
evidence: SingleSeedExplorationOptions.collocatesPerSeed() is parsed from the HTTP param 'nouns_per' (ExplorationHandlers:272) and assigned to 'nounsPerPredicate' in SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern (line 163).
The parameter controls Phase 2: for each collocate found for the seed, how many nouns to fetch via reverse lookup — i.e. it is 'nouns per collocate', not 'collocates per seed'.
The HTTP param name ('nouns_per') and implementation variable ('nounsPerPredicate') both correctly describe the concept; only the intermediate Java record field name is inverted.

issues.summary: ConlluConverter.processLines uses a generic verb that reveals nothing about its CoNLL-U → WPL chunk conversion behaviour
issues.suggestion: Rename to convertAndWriteConlluLines (or writeConlluAsWplChunks) to match the precision of the public method name convertConlluToWplChunks.
evidence: processLines (line 74) parses CoNLL-U token lines, emits <s>/<\/s> sentence markers, skips multi-word tokens and comments, accumulates token/sentence counts, and rotates WPL chunk files — none of this is hinted by the name.
The public entry point convertConlluToWplChunks correctly names the operation; the private helper should mirror that specificity.

issues.summary: BlackLabConllUIndexerTest is permanently @Disabled because the required YAML format config is not distributed with source, leaving the corpus indexing pipeline with zero automated tests
issues.suggestion: Extract a lightweight contract test for BlackLabConllUIndexer that does not depend on the format YAML: mock or stub the BlackLab DocumentFormats registration, or create a minimal in-memory format config that can be committed to the repo under src/test/resources. Alternatively, commit a stripped-down version of the YAML under src/test/resources to make the test runnable in CI.
evidence: @Disabled("Requires conllu-sentences.blf.yaml format config in the project root — not distributed with source") on the entire test class
The class comment explains the config registers CoNLL-U field mappings with BlackLab and is 'not distributed with the source because it contains site-specific field mappings'
ConlluConverter is tested in isolation (ConlluConverterTest) but the indexer that consumes its output is never exercised automatically

issues.summary: All live BlackLabQueryExecutor tests are @Disabled behind a CONCEPT_SKETCH_TEST_INDEX gate; the actual BlackLab query translation and result parsing is never exercised automatically
issues.suggestion: Introduce a small in-memory BlackLab index built from a committed 10-sentence CoNLL-U fixture under src/test/resources. Use BlackLab's existing test-index infrastructure (it supports creating indices programmatically) to make at least executeCollocations and executeSurfacePattern runnable in CI without external setup.
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutorTest.LiveIndex nested class is @Disabled; BlackLabQueryExecutorIntegrationTest and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and DependencySketchTest all carry @Disabled at class level
The non-disabled tests in BlackLabQueryExecutorTest only cover static helper methods (buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution) — not the actual BlackLab search execution
StubQueryExecutor returns empty lists for all operations; every test that exercises business logic bypasses the real query path entirely

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorerInternalTest accesses a package-private 5-param exploreByPattern overload directly, creating coupling to an implementation detail
issues.suggestion: Remove direct tests of the package-private overload and instead verify the deriveNounCqlConstraint behaviour through the public ExplorationService.exploreByPattern(seed, RelationConfig, SingleSeedExplorationOptions) interface. The RecordingExecutor pattern used in the test can still capture CQL patterns emitted through the public API call.
evidence: The test class Javadoc explicitly states: 'Tests for the package-private 5-param exploreByPattern overload. Lives in the exploration package to access package-private visibility.'

This means any refactor that changes the signature, visibility, or name of the internal overload will break these tests even if the public ExplorationService interface and its observable behaviour remain unchanged
deriveNounCqlConstraint behaviour (tested in two methods) is indirectly verifiable through the public exploreByPattern(seed, RelationConfig, opts) path

issues.summary: compareCollocateProfiles validates seeds.size() < 2 in both SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator
issues.suggestion: Decide which layer owns the precondition. If SemanticFieldExplorer is the public API surface and CollocateProfileComparator is a package-private collaborator, move the guard to SemanticFieldExplorer only and remove it from CollocateProfileComparator (add an Objects.requireNonNull instead). If the delegate is intended to be reusable independently, keep the guard there and remove the duplicate from the facade.
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.compareCollocateProfiles (line ~170): 'if (seedNouns.size() < 2) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("Comparison requires at least 2 seed nouns..."); }' then delegates to comparator.compareCollocateProfiles

CollocateProfileComparator.compareCollocateProfiles (line ~45): 'if (seedNouns.size() < 2) { throw new IllegalArgumentException("compareCollocateProfiles requires at least 2 seed nouns; got: ..."); }'

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery and getTotalFrequency are single-line pass-throughs to CollocateQueryHelper with no added logic
issues.suggestion: These methods exist to satisfy the QueryExecutor interface contract, which is fine. To reduce the pass-through feel, consider adding a brief Javadoc line on each noting that they are interface bridges to CollocateQueryHelper (e.g. '@implNote Delegates to {@link CollocateQueryHelper#executeBcqlQuery}'). Alternatively, if getTotalFrequency is only used via the interface for testing, consider whether it should stay on the interface or be made available through the concrete type instead.
evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor.executeBcqlQuery: 'return collocateQueryHelper.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlPattern, maxResults);' — zero added logic
BlackLabQueryExecutor.getTotalFrequency: 'return collocateQueryHelper.getTotalFrequency(lemma);' — zero added logic
By contrast, executeCollocations and executeSurfacePattern in the same class build BCQL strings and orchestrate multi-step frequency/group collection before delegating

issues.summary: api-layer HTTP handlers call RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern() directly, placing query-construction logic in the HTTP layer
issues.suggestion: Add a method to QueryExecutor (or a new thin SketchQueryService wrapper) that accepts a RelationConfig and headword and returns results — moving the RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern call inside that method. SketchHandlers and ConcordanceHandlers would then call the new method and be free of config imports. Alternatively, add a `executeSurfacePattern(RelationConfig, String, double, int)` overload to QueryExecutor so callers never construct the pattern string.
evidence: SketchHandlers line 206: `String fullPattern = RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern(rel, lemma);` then passes fullPattern directly to executor.executeSurfacePattern — the api layer is constructing the CQL string itself
SketchHandlers line 239: second identical call in a different execution branch (dependency sketch path)
ConcordanceHandlers line 59: `RelationPatternBuilder.buildFullPattern(rel.get(), req.seed())` passed to executor.executeSurfacePattern — same boundary violation in a third handler
RelationPatternBuilder is a config-layer utility; its correct callers should be below the api layer (e.g. in ExplorationService or a dedicated SketchQueryService)

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer convenience constructor names SemanticFieldExplorer concrete class, bypassing the ExplorationService interface
issues.suggestion: Move the `new SemanticFieldExplorer(...)` factory call to Main.java (which already constructs both executor and grammarConfig) and pass the SemanticFieldExplorer instance (typed as ExplorationService) to the full WordSketchApiServer constructor. This removes the concrete import from the api package entirely and keeps WordSketchApiServer free of exploration implementation knowledge.
evidence: WordSketchApiServer imports `pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.SemanticFieldExplorer` (concrete class) in addition to `ExplorationService`
Convenience constructor body: `this(executor, new SemanticFieldExplorer(executor, grammarConfig), port, grammarConfig)` — hardcodes the concrete type inside api
The full constructor signature accepts ExplorationService, showing the interface was intentionally introduced as a seam, but the production entry path re-couples to the concrete class

issues.summary: ExplorationHandlers imports GrammarConfig, RelationConfig, RelationUtils from config to resolve HTTP parameters, leaking config internals into the api layer
issues.suggestion: Introduce a RelationResolver helper (or extend ExplorationService) that accepts a relation-id string and returns an Optional<RelationConfig>. Move the alias-resolution and GrammarConfig lookup into that helper, which can live in the config or exploration package. ExplorationHandlers would then depend on the resolver interface rather than on GrammarConfig and RelationUtils directly. As a lighter alternative, add a single resolveRelation(String) method to GrammarConfig itself that subsumes the alias lookup, reducing the api's config imports to one type.
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig() calls RelationUtils.resolveRelationAlias() and grammarConfig.relation() — coupling api to alias-map internals defined in config
ExplorationHandlers constructor takes GrammarConfig directly, making config a required api-layer dependency
If relation alias logic changes (e.g. new canonical IDs added to RELATION_ALIASES), ExplorationHandlers must also be reviewed for impact
